summary: The Red Team Platform enables security admins to run active, multi-channel social engineering campaigns driven by AI-adaptive AEP chatbots. Admins select or build AEPs (Adversary Emulation Pathways — AI-powered scenario chatbots), compose multi-channel attack sequences in a node-based editor, configure approval-gated campaigns, and review employee conversation threads to classify complicit outcomes. Five sections under a new top-level Red Team nav item: Dashboard, AEP Library, Attack Library, Campaigns, Conversations.
in_scope: Top-level Red Team nav item with five sub-sections
AEP Library: card grid of 2 Dune-seeded AEPs (read-only), detail drawer with Workflow Steps / Branching Logic / Outcome tabs, Request AEP modal form
Attack Library: node-based editor scoped to linear sequences only (no branching)
Channel support: SMS (confirmed), Voice (human-logged), WhatsApp (tenant-provisioned), Viber (tenant-provisioned)
Campaign wizard: 5 steps — Details, Build Attack Flow (inline node editor), Audience, Remediation, Review + Request Start
Request-start campaign model with Red Team Approver role
Audience selection and phone coverage model (inherited from smishing)
Scheduling: start date, allowed days of week, start time, end time, timezone
Compliance acknowledgment checkbox at campaign submission
Remediation rule builder extended for complicity outcome trigger
Conversation management: cross-campaign thread table, thread detail, complicity/non-complicit marking with confirmation, reversal
Risk score update on complicity marking
Audit log on every complicity marking and reversal
out_of_scope: Customer self-serve AEP authoring (two-panel prompt builder) — not in v1; admins request AEPs from Dune via a form
AEP clone/edit — not in v1; all AEPs are Dune-managed
Live demo chat in AEP preview — not in v1; detail drawer is read-only
Automated voice call initiation and transcription
System-automated complicity detection (NLP flagging) — not confirmed for v1
Cross-section campaign view combining red team + smishing + email
Test send for red team campaigns
Approval notifications outside in-app and email

flow_steps.behavior: Admin opens the two-panel AEP builder. Writes a scenario prompt describing the attacker persona, goal, and tactics. Tests the AEP in the live chat simulator by playing the role of target employee. Adds refinement instructions to tune behavior. Iterates until satisfied. Clicks Save & Activate — content safety check runs. If passed, AEP becomes Active and available in campaign wizard.
Admin opens node-based canvas editor, drags channel nodes from palette, configures each node (message, delay), and saves. Locked nodes shown for unconfigured tenant channels.
Admin sets campaign name, start date, allowed sending days (multi-select), start time, end time, timezone.
states: validation-errors (past date, end before start, no days selected)

flow_steps.step: Campaign wizard — Step 2: Build Attack Flow
flow_steps.behavior: Admin lands on a node editor canvas with a pre-configured default flow: Initial Contact → (If replied: AEP Active) / (No reply after 3 days: Nudge → AEP Active / End). Admin taps Initial Contact to set channel and write opening message. Taps AEP node to select from available AEPs. Reviews/edits Nudge node delay and message. Can add up to 3 nudges via '+ Add nudge' affordance. Continue is blocked until all required fields are filled and an AEP is selected.
Admin selects groups/departments/cohorts. Phone coverage indicator shown. Hard block at 0% coverage.
Admin enables remediation rules triggered by complicity marking. Rules inherited and extended from smishing.

flow_steps.step: Campaign wizard — Step 5: Review + Request Start
flow_steps.behavior: Admin reviews summary, checks compliance checkbox, submits Request Campaign Start. Blocked if no approver configured or compliance unchecked.
Approver views pending campaign, approves or rejects with optional reason. Admin notified either way.
On start date, within allowed days and time window, campaign status changes to Active and attack orchestration begins.

Admin clicks Review on a Pending Review row. 480px right-anchored drawer opens showing: metadata row (Start Time, End Time, Phone Number, Recommended Status), full conversation transcript with attacker messages labeled 'Attacker · [AEP Name] · timestamp' and employee messages labeled '[Employee Name] · timestamp', AEP step indicators, classify section with radio buttons (Complicit / Non-complicit), reporting checkbox with optional notes, and sticky footer with Cancel + Apply updates. Apply updates is disabled until a radio is selected. No separate confirmation modal — radio selection + primary CTA is the confirmation pattern. Post-apply: drawer closes, table row updates, Reviewed by and Reviewed at columns populate.

Admin opens an already-classified thread. Drawer shows classification radio pre-selected. Admin changes radio selection and clicks Apply updates. No separate reversal modal — same Apply updates CTA handles reclassification. Change is logged in audit trail. Risk score adjustment triggered if moving from Complicit to Non-Complicit or vice versa.

components.notes: Dune Library tab: read-only actions only (Preview, Clone)
Action menu differs between Dune Library (Preview/Clone) and Custom (Edit/Clone/Archive)
Left: scenario prompt editor + refinement instructions. Right: live chat simulator (admin as target, AEP as attacker via LLM). See DS review section.
Linear sequences only v1; drag-to-add, click-to-configure, keyboard reorder
Opens on node click; channel-specific field sets
Must disable past dates
Multi-selectable; default Mon-Fri; min 1 required
Live demo chat where admin can interact with the AEP to preview behavior before selecting. Not a static script.
Dotted-grid background. Nodes: Initial Contact, AEP, Nudge, End. Arrows connect nodes top-to-bottom. Branches auto-generate from each contact node. Pre-configured default flow on load.
Channel chips: SMS / WhatsApp / Telegram. Locked chips for unconfigured tenant channels.
Shows AEP name + method after selection. Green accent border. Always terminal on replied branches.

components.component: Card + number input + channel pill selector + Textarea
components.notes: Delay: number input + 'days' label. Max 3 nudge nodes in v1. '+ Add nudge' dashed card below last nudge.
No redesign needed
Inherited from smishing; pre-built rule cards extended
Shown only when no approver is configured

components.overrides: Label: Request Campaign Start (not Launch Campaign)
components.notes: Disabled until compliance + approver both satisfied

components.overrides: Right-anchored; triggered from table Review button
components.notes: Header: '[Employee Name] conversation history' + X close. Metadata row below header. Transcript. Classify section with RadioGroup. Reporting section with Checkbox + Textarea. Sticky footer.

components.overrides: 4 fields: Start Time, End Time, Phone Number, Recommended Status
components.notes: Recommended Status shows system-derived FAIL/PASS badge + category tag. Hidden when no system flag.
No default selection for Pending Review threads. Pre-selected to current status for already-classified threads. Enabling 'Apply updates' requires selection or change.

components.overrides: Textarea visible only when checkbox checked
components.notes: Helper text: 'Includes conversation, classification, and optional notes.' Max 500 chars.
Disabled until radio is selected (or changed from existing). No separate confirmation modal — this IS the confirmation step.

interactions: Node canvas: drag channel chip from palette to canvas to add a step; drag node within canvas to reorder; click node to open config drawer; delete key on focused node triggers confirmation if node has content
VEP script editor: click Add exchange to append a new node; click delete icon on node to remove (confirmation if has content); drag to reorder nodes (v1: may defer reorder to keyboard-only)
Compliance checkbox at Step 5: checking enables the Request Campaign Start CTA (if approver also configured)

Complicity marking: click Review on table row → drawer opens → read transcript → select Complicit or Non-complicit radio → optionally check Send report + add notes → click Apply updates → drawer closes → table row updates, Reviewed by + Reviewed at columns populate, toast shown, risk score queued

Complicity reclassification (reversal): open classified thread drawer → radio pre-selected → change radio selection → Apply updates enabled → click Apply updates → reclassification saved, audit entry added, risk score adjusted
Bulk complicity marking: select rows via checkboxes → Mark selected as... dropdown → Complicit/Non-Complicit → confirmation modal with count
Request Start: click CTA → campaign status → Pending Approval → toast → redirect to campaign detail
Approval: approver clicks Approve → campaign → Approved status → admin notified
Rejection: approver clicks Reject → enters reason (optional) → campaign → Draft → admin notified with reason
Withdraw request: admin clicks Withdraw Request → campaign → Draft → approver notified

rbac.disabled_tooltip: Confirm the compliance statement before requesting start.
No Red Team Approver is configured. Go to Settings → Red Team → Configure Approver.
Only designated Red Team Approvers can approve campaigns.
You don't have permission to mark conversations. Contact your administrator.
You don't have permission to create VEPs. Contact your administrator.
You don't have permission to create attack sequences. Contact your administrator.
You don't have permission to view employee details. Contact your administrator.
This VEP is used by [N] active campaigns.
This channel is not configured for your organization. Contact your admin to enable [Channel].

accessibility_requirements: Node canvas: all operations achievable by keyboard (Tab to focus nodes, Enter to open config, Delete to remove, arrow keys to reorder)
Conversation thread table: rows announce 'Employee [Name], Campaign [Name], Status [Status]' to screen readers
Complicity confirmation modal: focus moves to modal on open; Escape = Cancel (not Confirm); Tab order Cancel → Confirm; focus returns correctly on close
AEP chat simulator: Tab to focus input, Enter to send; new AEP messages announced to screen reader: 'Attacker: [text]'; typing indicator announced as 'AEP is responding'
Day-of-week toggle chips: role=checkbox; announces selected state per chip ('Monday selected', 'Tuesday not selected')
AEP preview drawer (live chat): chat messages announced in order; attacker messages as 'Attacker: [text]'; typing indicator announced as 'AEP is responding'
Status badges: text label always present; color is supplemental only
Mark as Complicit button (color/feedback/danger): confirm text label maintains ≥4.5:1 contrast ratio against danger background token
acceptance_criteria: Given any page in the app, when the user navigates to Red Team in the side-nav, then Dashboard loads as the default sub-view
Given threads in Pending Review status, when the user views the Red Team nav, then Conversations sub-nav shows the correct pending count badge
Given the AEP Library loads, then exactly the Dune-seeded AEPs are shown as cards in a 2-column grid
Given the admin clicks View details on an AEP card, then the 480px detail drawer opens showing Workflow Steps, Branching Logic, and Outcome tabs
Given the Outcome tab bar, when the admin clicks Complicit, then the complicit criteria are shown; when they click Non-Complicit, the non-complicit criteria replace them
Given the admin clicks Request AEP, then a modal opens with the request form; submitting the form shows a success message with a 5-business-day SLA
Given the AEP Library is empty (no seeded AEPs), then an empty state is shown with contact text — no self-serve creation CTA
Given a tenant without WhatsApp provisioned, when the user opens the node editor, then the WhatsApp chip is locked and cannot be dragged onto the canvas
Given an attack sequence with unsaved changes, when the user navigates away, then a confirmation modal appears before navigation proceeds
Given Step 5 of the campaign wizard with no Red Team Approver configured, then Request Campaign Start is disabled and a warning callout links to Settings
Given the compliance checkbox is unchecked, when the user attempts Request Campaign Start, then the button remains disabled
Given 0% phone coverage for the selected audience, when the user reaches Step 3, then Continue is disabled with a resolution path shown
Given a valid campaign submission, when the user clicks Request Campaign Start, then status → Pending Approval, approver notified, admin sees success toast
Given a campaign in Pending Approval, when the Approver clicks Approve, then status → Approved and admin receives notification
Given a campaign in Pending Approval, when the Approver rejects with a reason, then status → Draft and admin receives notification with reason
Given an employee thread in Pending Review, when the admin opens the drawer, then Apply updates is disabled until a radio selection is made
Given the admin selects Complicit radio and clicks Apply updates, then thread status → Complicit, Reviewed by and Reviewed at columns populate, risk score updated, remediation triggered, toast shown
Given the admin selects Non-complicit radio and clicks Apply updates, then thread status → Non-Complicit, Reviewed by and Reviewed at columns populate, no risk score change, toast shown
Given a Complicit thread, when the admin opens the drawer and changes the radio to Non-complicit and clicks Apply updates, then reclassification is saved, audit entry created, risk score adjusted
Given a thread with no employee reply, then thread status must be No Response — not Pending Review
Given an employee who replied STOP, when the system processes the reply, then employee is removed from send queue, opt-out record created, thread status → Opted Out
Given a view-only admin, when they view a conversation thread, then marking buttons are absent and employee PII is masked

open_questions.question: Risk score delta for complicity: what is the impact, and does it replace or augment the smishing model?
Red Team Approver RBAC: new permission level or flag on existing admin role?
Thread model for non-SMS channels: how are inbound replies associated with campaign threads for WhatsApp, Viber, and Voice?
SOC/IR lead PII access in thread view: full PII or redacted?
LLM infrastructure for AEP conversations: which model, hosting, latency SLA, prompt injection guardrails?
Customer AEP authoring scope: two-panel builder in initial launch or post-launch? Determines AEP Library build scope.
AEP prompt content guardrails: what is blocked, what triggers a warning, what is always permitted?
System-suggested complicity detection (NLP flagging): in scope for v1?
Dashboard data contracts: time window, metrics, refresh cadence?
Allowed sending days + approval timing: if approved on a non-allowed day, does it auto-activate on next allowed day?
Channel provider availability at launch: which tenants have WhatsApp/Viber and what is the provisioning flow?
Complicity reversal: available to any Security Admin, or only the admin who made the original marking?
Bulk complicity marking: is there a cap on batch size?

dependencies: Red Team Approver RBAC role must be created or extended in the Dune permission model
Risk scoring system must support a new complicity signal from red_team_complicit_marked
Audit log system must record complicity marking and reversal events
SMS, WhatsApp, Viber channel provider integrations must be tenant-provisionable
Voice call logging endpoint (human-executed, v1) must accept operator-submitted transcript/notes
Dune must seed a AEP library at tenant creation — empty library hard-blocks campaign creation
Phone number storage and coverage model (from smishing) must be operational before red team campaigns can be targeted
new_ds_patterns: Two-panel AEP builder — prompt editor (left) + live LLM chat simulator (right) — requires DS review
AEP preview drawer (live demo chat) — interactive LLM chat in 480px drawer — requires DS review
Node-based canvas editor (linear) — drag-to-add, click-to-configure attack sequence — requires DS review
Day-of-week toggle chip group — multi-selectable days — requires DS review
inherited_components: Phone coverage indicator + coverage drawer — smishing wizard Step 1
CSV upload drawer — smishing wizard Step 1
Cooldown warning callout — smishing wizard Step 1
Compliance acknowledgment checkbox — smishing wizard Step 7 (copy updated)
Remediation rule builder + module selector — smishing wizard Step 5
Date/time picker + timezone selector — smishing wizard Step 4
Wizard step bar — smishing wizard (6-step variant)
